mechanism: The Taylor Rule coefficient (phi_pi) determines how effectively the central bank offsets demand shocks. High phi_pi → demand shocks fully offset → only supply shocks reach asset prices → negative correlation. Low phi_pi → demand shocks partially transmitted → mixed shock correlation → positive correlation.
regime_dependence: CB credibility is necessary but not sufficient for negative correlation. Even a credible CB cannot produce negative correlation if supply shocks dominate the macro environment (e.g., 2022 under a credible Fed).
